answer:
\boxed{B}

Chichén Itzá is one of the most famous Mayan cities, flourishing during the **Postclassic period** (around 900–1200 CE). While the Toltecs (Option A) influenced its architecture, the site was primarily built and inhabited by the **Mayans**. The Incas (Option C) and Aztecs (Option D) were later civilizations with no connection to this region.
